title: Product Launch Campaign
description: Create a launch campaign for a new consumer product.
template: [S] We are launching a new organic skincare serum for women aged 25-35 in urban markets. [H] You are a digital marketing strategist. [A] Create a 2-week product launch campaign plan. [R] Include social media, email, and influencer activities. Keep the tone modern and brand-focused. [P] Present the output as a table with channel, activity, objective, and timeline.

title: Instagram Caption Set
description: Generate short promotional captions for social media.
template: [S] A wellness brand is promoting a new mindfulness app for working professionals. [H] You are a social media copywriter. [A] Write 5 Instagram captions for the launch. [R] Keep each caption under 150 characters and include 3 relevant hashtags. [P] Present the output as a numbered list.

title: Remote Work Policy
description: Draft a structured HR policy document.
template: [S] A growing technology company is formalizing remote work for employees across multiple locations. [H] You are an HR policy specialist. [A] Draft a remote work policy. [R] Include eligibility, attendance expectations, communication norms, performance expectations, and security compliance. [P] Present the output with headings and bullet points.

title: Employee Engagement Survey
description: Create HR survey questions for employee engagement.
template: [S] Our company wants to measure employee engagement after a major restructuring. [H] You are an organizational development expert. [A] Create 12 employee engagement survey questions. [R] Include a mix of rating-scale and open-ended questions. Keep the tone neutral and professional. [P] Present the questions in a numbered list with the question type clearly marked.

title: Budget Analysis
description: Explain and analyze a budgeting approach.
template: [S] I am teaching budgeting techniques to MBA students. [H] You are a finance professor. [A] Explain zero-based budgeting with a practical example. [R] Include definition, advantages, disadvantages, and a short business example. Keep it under 400 words. [P] Present the output in four labeled sections.

title: Investment Recommendation Memo
description: Draft a financial recommendation memo.
template: [S] A mid-sized company is evaluating whether to invest in a new software platform. [H] You are a corporate finance analyst. [A] Write an investment recommendation memo. [R] Include cost considerations, expected benefits, financial risks, and payback logic. Keep the tone formal and executive-ready. [P] Present the output as a memo with recommendation at the end.

title: Market Entry Strategy
description: Develop a strategy for entering a new market.
template: [S] A SaaS company wants to expand into Southeast Asia. [H] You are a business strategy consultant. [A] Create a market entry strategy. [R] Include customer segment, go-to-market model, risks, and competitive considerations. [P] Present the output as a strategic framework with short sections and bullet points.

title: SWOT-Based Strategic Plan
description: Use SWOT analysis to guide strategy.
template: [S] A retail brand is facing strong online competition and wants to reposition itself. [H] You are a strategic management expert. [A] Create a strategic response using SWOT analysis. [R] Include strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats, and 5 recommended actions. [P] Present the output as a SWOT table followed by an action plan.

title: Supply Chain Improvement
description: Identify ways to improve operational efficiency.
template: [S] A manufacturing company is facing delivery delays and rising logistics costs. [H] You are an operations consultant. [A] Suggest 5 ways to improve supply chain efficiency. [R] Focus on practical, implementable actions and mention possible trade-offs. [P] Present the output as a numbered recommendation list.

title: Process Improvement Plan
description: Improve an internal business process.
template: [S] A customer support department has long response times and inconsistent service quality. [H] You are a process improvement specialist. [A] Create a process improvement plan. [R] Include bottlenecks, root causes, and improvement steps. [P] Present the output with sections for problem, diagnosis, and recommendations.

title: Leadership Development Advice
description: Create leadership improvement guidance.
template: [S] A newly promoted team leader is struggling to motivate a cross-functional team. [H] You are a leadership coach. [A] Provide 5 strategies to improve team motivation and trust. [R] Use simple language, practical steps, and one example for each strategy. [P] Present the output in a numbered format.

title: Conflict Resolution Guidance
description: Help leaders resolve workplace tension.
template: [S] Two department managers are in conflict over resource allocation, affecting team morale. [H] You are an executive coach specializing in conflict resolution. [A] Suggest a leadership approach to resolve the issue. [R] Include communication tactics, meeting structure, and follow-up. [P] Present the output as a 3-phase action plan.

title: Client Onboarding Email
description: Write a professional onboarding message.
template: [S] A consulting firm has signed a new corporate client. [H] You are a business communication expert. [A] Write a client onboarding email. [R] Keep the tone warm, professional, and concise. Mention next steps and expected timeline. [P] Present the output as a polished email draft with subject line.

title: Executive Summary
description: Condense a business report into key highlights.
template: [S] A senior manager needs a short summary of a long internal project report. [H] You are an executive communication specialist. [A] Write a one-page executive summary. [R] Focus on key findings, risks, and recommendations. Avoid technical jargon. [P] Present the output with headings and bullet points.

title: Customer Churn Analysis
description: Structure a data interpretation prompt.
template: [S] A telecom company has customer churn data and wants insight into retention drivers. [H] You are a business analyst. [A] Analyze likely churn factors and recommend actions. [R] Use a data-driven tone and include assumptions clearly. [P] Present the output as insights followed by recommendations.

title: KPI Dashboard Interpretation
description: Interpret business KPIs for management.
template: [S] A retail manager is reviewing monthly KPIs including revenue, conversion, churn, and customer satisfaction. [H] You are a business intelligence analyst. [A] Interpret the KPI trends and identify 3 action priorities. [R] Keep it concise and practical for decision-making. [P] Present the output as a short executive briefing.

title: Project Plan Outline
description: Create a structured project plan.
template: [S] A company is implementing a new CRM system over 3 months. [H] You are a project manager. [A] Create a project plan outline. [R] Include milestones, stakeholders, risks, and dependencies. [P] Present the output as a table with timeline and ownership.

title: Risk Register Prompt
description: Generate a risk register for a business project.
template: [S] A digital transformation initiative is being launched across multiple departments. [H] You are a project risk manager. [A] Create a risk register. [R] Include risk description, probability, impact, mitigation, and owner. [P] Present the output as a structured table.

title: Startup Idea Evaluation
description: Evaluate a new venture idea.
template: [S] An entrepreneur wants to validate a subscription-based mental wellness platform for students. [H] You are a startup mentor and business model advisor. [A] Evaluate the startup idea. [R] Assess problem-solution fit, market demand, risks, and monetization. [P] Present the output as a balanced evaluation with strengths, risks, and next steps.

title: Business Diagnostic Prompt
description: Diagnose a company problem like a consultant.
template: [S] A mid-sized retail company is seeing declining profitability despite stable sales. [H] You are a management consultant. [A] Diagnose possible causes and propose next steps. [R] Consider pricing, cost structure, customer behavior, and operations. [P] Present the output as a diagnosis followed by recommendations.
